images.caption: Figure 1:Overall architecture.Each RGB frame𝐈j\mathbf{I}_{j}and its reconstruction pointmap𝐏j​(tj)\mathbf{P}_{j}(t_{j})are encoded into RGB and pointmap latents using separate VAE encoders. A geometry latent is formed by channel-wise concatenation, and a track latent replicates the first-frame geometry latent across all frames. The latents are concatenated along the token dimension and processed by a video DiT, where RoPE assigns the same temporal index to each frame. The track latent outputs are decoded using separate VAE decoders into a residual track𝚫^j\hat{\mathbf{\Delta}}_{j}and visibility𝐨^j\hat{\mathbf{o}}_{j}.

Figure 2:Pointmap Representations.Given 3D points of a dynamic scene in world coordinates, the reconstruction pointmap represents 3D points of𝐈j\mathbf{I}_{j}’s content attjt_{j}, while the tracking pointmap represents 3D points of𝐈0\mathbf{I}_{0}(reference frame) attjt_{j}, so that all 3D points of𝐈0\mathbf{I}_{0}are tracked across time.

Figure 3:Query-key attention visualization.The query point is marked as a green circle. (a) Attention from track latents𝐫5\mathbf{r}_{5}at timestampt5t_{5}to geometry latents{𝐠j}j=0F\{\mathbf{g}_{j}\}_{j=0}^{F}. Attention is predominantly localized on𝐠5\mathbf{g}_{5}, showing that RoPE correctly assigns a target timestamp to each track latent.
(b) Within𝐠5\mathbf{g}_{5}, attention aligns with the same physical point under motion, demonstrating accurate dense correspondence between track and geometry latents.
Figure 4:Qualitative comparison on ITTO[10]videos.TrackCraft3R accurately estimates dense 3D trajectories on real-world videos under large object dynamics and occlusion.
Figure 5:Robustness to large inter-frame motion (a, b) and long videos (c, d).TrackCraft3R’s performance drops much more slowly thanDELTAv2as stridessor frame lengthLLgrows.

Attention is concentrated on the temporally aligned geometry latent𝐠5\mathbf{g}_{5}(29.0%), showing that RoPE correctly assigns a target timestamp to each track latent.

We visualize attention from the track latent𝐫5\mathbf{r}_{5}at timestampt5t_{5}to geometry latents{𝐠j}j=0F\{\mathbf{g}_{j}\}_{j=0}^{F}across transformer layers. Attention is concentrated on the temporally aligned geometry latent𝐠5\mathbf{g}_{5}(30.6%), showing that RoPE correctly assigns a target timestamp to each track latent.

Figure 8:Query–key attention visualization on PStudio[31].The query point is marked with a green circle. Attention between the temporally aligned track and geometry latents identifies accurate correspondences of the same physical points in specific layers (highlighted in red boxes).

Figure 9:Query–key attention visualization on ADT[56].The query point is marked with a green circle. Attention between the temporally aligned track and geometry latents identifies accurate correspondences of the same physical points in specific layers (highlighted in red boxes).
Figure 10:Qualitative results on ITTO[10]videos.TrackCraft3R accurately estimates dense 3D trajectories on real-world videos under large camera motion, object dynamics and occlusion.

Figure 11:Qualitative comparison on ITTO[10]and DAVIS[57]videos.TrackCraft3R accurately estimates dense 3D trajectories on real-world videos under large camera motion, object motion, and occlusion. Note that the same query points are shared across methods.
